item.title: Last Thursday marked the completion of the comprehensive and sensitive restorati...
item.description:
@darkmattersstudio

Last Thursday marked the completion of the comprehensive and sensitive restoration of the Court Theatre at Christiansborg, and the unveiling of the new exhibitions and interpretive elements. The occasion was celebrated in the presence of H.M. Queen Margrethe and H.M. Queen Mary.

As part of the project, JAC Studios, Dark Matters, and Fortheloveoflight have, through a close interdisciplinary collaboration, developed a new interpretive approach in which architecture, light, sound, and storytelling are conceived as a single, unified experience. With respect for the listed building, the Court Theatre has been activated both as a historical artifact and as a living stage.

This has been a rare and privileged project at the intersection of restoration, performing arts, interpretation, and design, realized in close collaboration with the Theatre Museum at the Court Theatre, Slots og kulturstyrelsen, foundations, and a strong team of specialists.

The Theatre Museum at the Court Theatre will reopen to the public on Saturday, 7 March 2026.

Thank you
@teatermuseet
@augustinusfonden
@apmollerfonde
@aageogjohannelouishansenfonden

Photo by
Tine Bek
and
Kongehuset.dk
item.content:encoded:
@darkmattersstudio

Last Thursday marked the completion of the comprehensive and sensitive restoration of the Court Theatre at Christiansborg, and the unveiling of the new exhibitions and interpretive elements. The occasion was celebrated in the presence of H.M. Queen Margrethe and H.M. Queen Mary.

As part of the project, JAC Studios, Dark Matters, and Fortheloveoflight have, through a close interdisciplinary collaboration, developed a new interpretive approach in which architecture, light, sound, and storytelling are conceived as a single, unified experience. With respect for the listed building, the Court Theatre has been activated both as a historical artifact and as a living stage.

This has been a rare and privileged project at the intersection of restoration, performing arts, interpretation, and design, realized in close collaboration with the Theatre Museum at the Court Theatre, Slots og kulturstyrelsen, foundations, and a strong team of specialists.

The Theatre Museum at the Court Theatre will reopen to the public on Saturday, 7 March 2026.

Thank you
@teatermuseet
@augustinusfonden
@apmollerfonde
@aageogjohannelouishansenfonden

Photo by
Tine Bek
and
Kongehuset.dk
item.author: darkmattersstudio

item.title: We are looking for a new colleague! About you: Experience with After Effects and...
item.description:
@darkmattersstudio

We are looking for a new colleague!

About you:
Experience with After Effects and the Adobe suite is a requirement.
You must also have worked with 3D software, preferably Houdini or similar, to a degree where you have both experience with animation, rendering and possibly modelling.

Interested in art, history, design, motion graphics and general graphic design.

Experience in Touch Designer, Unity, Unreal programs and general interactive design will be considered a great advantage.
If you have other qualities/skills that fit our profile, we will always include them as opportunities in future projects.

You will get to work on a wide range of exciting projects, where you often dive into areas you have never been before. We run a very flat structure in the studio, i.e. we are all together from start to finish on almost all tasks. The digital is often the cornerstone of most tasks. Still, research on how to take over an entire concert hall with plastic ivy, build a 4m high iceberg or physically find all the furniture from Hammershøi's pictures, is not unusual in our everyday life.

About Dark Matters
We are a creative studio that creates unique universes and visually aesthetic experiences through light, architecture, storytelling and technology.
Dark Matters works across media with exhibition design, installations, concert visuals and projections – in artistic as well as commercial arenas.
We are a small team with a busy but informal working environment in the middle of Nørrebro / Copenhagen.

Read more about us at www.darkmatters.dk and send a short application with attached CV and visual portfolio to Lasse Andersen at lasse@darkmatters.dk
item.content:encoded:
@darkmattersstudio

We are looking for a new colleague!

About you:
Experience with After Effects and the Adobe suite is a requirement.
You must also have worked with 3D software, preferably Houdini or similar, to a degree where you have both experience with animation, rendering and possibly modelling.

Interested in art, history, design, motion graphics and general graphic design.

Experience in Touch Designer, Unity, Unreal programs and general interactive design will be considered a great advantage.
If you have other qualities/skills that fit our profile, we will always include them as opportunities in future projects.

You will get to work on a wide range of exciting projects, where you often dive into areas you have never been before. We run a very flat structure in the studio, i.e. we are all together from start to finish on almost all tasks. The digital is often the cornerstone of most tasks. Still, research on how to take over an entire concert hall with plastic ivy, build a 4m high iceberg or physically find all the furniture from Hammershøi's pictures, is not unusual in our everyday life.

About Dark Matters
We are a creative studio that creates unique universes and visually aesthetic experiences through light, architecture, storytelling and technology.
Dark Matters works across media with exhibition design, installations, concert visuals and projections – in artistic as well as commercial arenas.
We are a small team with a busy but informal working environment in the middle of Nørrebro / Copenhagen.

Read more about us at www.darkmatters.dk and send a short application with attached CV and visual portfolio to Lasse Andersen at lasse@darkmatters.dk
item.author: darkmattersstudio

item.title: The new website is up now with a bunch of new projects! https://darkmatters.dk/
item.description:
@darkmattersstudio

The new website is up now with a bunch of new projects!

https://darkmatters.dk/
